beats.narration_text: Konnichiwa — Liam, in for Bear. kids-video: Bear's Cubs, early-childhood concept films for ages one to five. One episode, one concept — a color, a shape, a number. Nine research-derived laws. Gate K validates the beat sheet before any audio is generated. Never bypassed.

props.runningText: plan → Gate K → audio → visuals → compile...
output: One episode = ONE concept (color, shape, number)
Gate K (kids_gate.py) validates before any audio spend

beats.narration_text: Anatomy. One host — the Bear Brown mascot, camera-address, second person. One concept per episode. Beat roles: hello, anchor, teach, question, pause, confirm, recap, contrast, song, return, co-view. The pause beat is real silence — no music sting, no cutaway. Geoplates — big flat shapes on cream — are the house visual style. The design rules are not style preferences; each traces to a developmental-psychology finding.

props.title: One concept, one episode, nine research laws.
files: SKILL.md — nine laws + beat roles + Gate K
reference/pedagogy.md — developmental-psych sources for each law
reference/characters.md — closed host-pose library (8 named poses)
scripts/kids_gate.py — Gate K validator (runs before audio)

beats.narration_text: Pipeline. Phase one: write the beat sheet per the nine laws. Phase two: run Gate K — kids gate dot py must pass before any audio; fix the sheet, never annotate around a failure. Phase three: generate audio — pause beats generate as real silence, crediting only the spoken beats. Phase four: visuals — geoplate SVGs or Manim per beat. Phase five: compile — the slot contract, Gates A and W, mascot host beats composite from the bearbrown folder.

props.title: Plan → Gate K → audio → visuals → compile.

phases.label: PLAN
phases.note: beat_sheet.json: concept, label_word, exemplars, pause triad, co-view close — 9 laws

phases.label: GATE K
phases.note: python3 scripts/kids_gate.py <episode> — must PASS; fix sheet, never annotate around failure

phases.label: AUDIO
phases.note: generate_audio.py — pause beats = silent=true; spoken beats only spend credits

phases.label: VISUALS
phases.note: geoplate SVGs (big flat shapes on cream) / Manim per beat; Gates A + W apply

phases.label: COMPILE
phases.note: compile.py slot contract; mascot composites from bearbrown/; review MP4 for human

props.footerNote: Never bypass Gate K. An episode that fails pedagogy does not get a voice.

beats.narration_text: Self-demo. Phase one is purely free — no audio, no video. Watch Claude write a Gate K-valid beat sheet for a circle episode, ages one to three. One concept, four exemplars, one pause beat, one contrast case, one co-view close.

props.runningText: concept → exemplars → triad → contrast → Gate K check...
output: Concept: circle | Age band: 1-3 | Label word: 'circle'

beats.narration_text: B00 hello: Bear Brown, wave pose. B01 anchor: pure circle swatch, static background. B02 teach: red apple — label: circle. B03 teach: yellow sun — label: circle. B04 teach: blue ball — label: circle. B05 question: orange clock — new exemplar — 'Is this a circle?' B06 pause: silent, three seconds — no sting, no cutaway. B07 confirm: 'Yes! That's a circle!' B08 contrast: green square — 'This is NOT a circle.' B09 recap: 'circle, circle, circle' — voice only, no new visuals. B10 song: music cue, under fifteen seconds. B11 return: target card. B12 co-view: 'Find something round in your room and show a grown-up.' Gate K checks: four exemplars varying size and shape — pass; label word circle verbatim — pass; one contrast case — pass; pause silent — pass; song under fifteen seconds — pass; total under four minutes — pass.

props.title: Phase 1 — 'circle' episode, ages 1-3 (Gate K-valid)
props.code:
CONCEPT: circle  |  AGE BAND: 1-3  |  LABEL WORD: "circle"

B00  hello    Bear Brown, wave   "Do you see a circle?"
B01  anchor   pure circle swatch  "Circle." (×2)
B02  teach    red apple          "Apple. Circle!"
B03  teach    yellow sun         "Sun. Circle!"
B04  teach    blue ball          "Ball. Circle!"
B05  question orange clock (NEW) "Is this a circle?"
B06  pause    silent=true  3s    —
B07  confirm  same clock         "Yes! That's a circle!"
B08  contrast green square       "This is NOT a circle."
B09  recap    voice only         "Circle... circle... circle."
B10  song     music cue <15s     "Circles, circles everywhere…"
B11  return   target card        "Circle."
B12  coview   Bear, bye pose     "Find something round and show a grown-up!"

GATE K:
  ✓ 4 exemplars (apple/sun/ball/clock) vary on size+shape
  ✓ label_word 'circle' verbatim every time
  ✓ 1 contrast case   ✓ pause silent=true   ✓ song <15s
  ✓ total ~65s < 4:00 cap

beats.narration_text: Mechanism one. The pause is the mechanism; never fill it. The contingency triad — question, then a real pause beat of two to four seconds, then confirm — is the core of how a toddler learns a label. The pause is not dead air to clean up; it is the space where the child attempts the label. A music sting or a cutaway destroys the mechanism entirely. The confirm works whether or not the child answered — 'Yes! That's a circle!' is right either way.

props.heading: Question → silence → confirm. The silence is the mechanism.
props.body: Law 3 — Contingency triad: question beat (new exemplar not seen before) → pause beat ('silent': true, 2–4s, NO music, NO cutaway, NOTHING) → confirm beat ('Yes! That's a circle!' — works regardless of child response). The pause beat is generate_audio's 'silent': true — it generates actual silence, not narration. Spending credits only on spoken beats.
props.quote: The pause is the mechanism; never fill it.

beats.narration_text: Mechanism two. Silence is correct design, not a gap. The music discipline law allows music in exactly one dedicated song beat, under fifteen seconds, reused verbatim across the whole series. Teaching beats are unscored. Pause beats are unscored. The absence of music during concept instruction is not a production gap to be fixed — it is the correct condition for a young child learning a label. Incidental music competes with the voice; it is removed by design.

props.heading: Music in one beat. Teaching beats are silent.
props.body: Law 5 — Music discipline: music appears ONLY in the song beat (≤15s), reused verbatim across all episodes (a retrieval cue, not a new song per episode). Teaching beats and pause beats are unscored — no background music. The beat sheet enforces this: any beat with role≠song that carries a music_cue fails Gate K. The song beat is the musical moment; the rest of the episode is clean.
props.quote: silence is correct design, not a gap.

beats.narration_text: Mechanism three. Never bypass Gate K. An episode that fails pedagogy does not get a voice. Gate K runs kids gate dot py against the beat sheet before any audio is generated. It checks all nine laws: concept map for age band, exemplar count and variability, pause triad presence, contrast case, music discipline, co-view close, runtime cap. A failure is a hard stop — fix the sheet, not the gate check.

props.heading: Gate K is a hard stop, not a suggestion.
props.body: scripts/kids_gate.py validates: concept fits age-band map ✓, 3–5 exemplars varying on non-target dims ✓, label_word verbatim every time ✓, 1 contrast case ✓, pause beat silent=true ✓, song beat ≤15s ✓, co-view close ✓, runtime ≤ age-band cap ✓. Any failure: fix the beat sheet. Never annotate around a failure. An episode that fails pedagogy does not get audio.
props.quote: Never bypass Gate K. An episode that fails pedagogy does not get a voice.

beats.narration_text: Verdict. Claude, Cubs. kids-video delivers early-childhood concept films where every design decision is a research constraint: one concept, real pause beats, exemplar variability, one contrast case, a co-view close. Gate K validates before any voice is spent.

artifactLines: one concept per episode — color, shape, or number, not a mix
contingency triad: question → real silence → confirm
music in one beat only — teaching beats are unscored by design
Gate K runs first: 9 laws checked before any audio is generated

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Run kids-video for any one concept and age band. Two questions before you start: what is the one concept — not a theme, one label word? And have you run Gate K and confirmed it passes before generating any audio?
